instruction:
Let the continuous function $f(x)$ satisfy: $f(x) - \int_{0}^{x} f(x-t) \, dt = e^x$, then
$f(x) = ()$.

Options:
A. $$\left( x^2+1 \right) \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
B. $$\left( x+1 \right) \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
C. $$\left( x+1 \right)^2 \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
D. $$\left( 2x+1 \right) \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
E. $$\left( x-2 \right) \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
F. $$\left( x+2 \right) \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
G. $$\left( x+3 \right) \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
H. $$\left( x+5 \right) \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
I. $$\left( x-1 \right) \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
J. $$\left( x+4 \right) \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
